source: Pre-trained Language Model for Web-scale Retrieval in Baidu Search

concepts.finding: Baidu uses dual-encoder architecture for web-scale retrieval: query encoder + document encoder, both initialized from pre-trained ERNIE. Key contributions: (1) knowledge distillation from cross-attention ranker to dual-encoder, (2) hard negative mining via teacher ranker, (3) asynchronous index refresh for fresh content. 8% improvement in recall@K.
concepts.implication: Knowledge distillation from cross-attention to dual-encoder can improve keyword embedding quality: train a cross-attention teacher (slow, accurate) on keyword-intent pairs, distill to dual-encoder student (fast, deployment-ready). Hard negatives from teacher improve embedding discrimination.
concepts.open_question: Can cross-attention distillation improve keyword embedding quality for intent clustering? What teacher-student architecture is appropriate?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: Knowledge distillation from a cross-attention keyword-intent model to a dual-encoder improves intent matching recall by 5%+ while maintaining embedding retrieval latency.
